behaviors.description: Out-of-stock products are excluded even if they have high relevance scores.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the handoff between inventory availability filtering and ranking output (MOCK-MISS yes for module interaction). No real OS/network/subprocess is required (REAL-INFRA no), and no AI/ML output dependency (LLM-DEP no). Rejected System because behavior text is about catalog/filter logic, not real infrastructure effects.
behaviors.test_description: Run recommendation generation with an in-process catalog/repository containing both in-stock and out-of-stock items where out-of-stock items have higher relevance scores; assert final returned list contains only in-stock products.

behaviors.description: Blocked categories in user settings are excluded from recommendations.
behaviors.justification: Bug appears when user-settings module and filtering module are wired together (MOCK-MISS yes for boundary handoff). No real infra is required (REAL-INFRA no), no model-output-quality dependency (LLM-DEP no). Rejected Unit because behavior explicitly depends on settings-to-filter integration.
behaviors.test_description: Create a user profile with blocked categories and a mixed-category product set; execute recommendation pipeline and assert blocked categories are absent in results.

behaviors.description: Products matching recent browsing patterns rank higher than products with no browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text targets ranking rule correctness inside the recommendation algorithm itself; this can fail within one scoring/ranking function without cross-module I/O (MOCK-MISS no). No real infra needed and no AI model call implied. Rejected Integration because no concrete boundary dependency is required by this behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Call the ranking/scoring function with controlled feature vectors where two products differ only by browsing-pattern signal; assert the browsing-matched product receives a higher score/rank.

behaviors.description: Pagination returns correct page with stable ordering across repeated page-2 requests when data is unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is often at module boundary between ranked result source and pagination layer (state/sort handoff), not just slice math (MOCK-MISS yes). No real OS/network required (REAL-INFRA no). Rejected System because behavior text constrains unchanged data and ordering logic, not external infra behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Generate ranked results through the service with a fixed dataset, request page 2 twice with identical inputs, and assert item set/order are identical and match expected offset/limit.

behaviors.description: At least one recommendation comes from an unbrowsed category (serendipity).
behaviors.justification: This is a ranking-policy invariant that can be validated in the algorithm output without external module wiring (MOCK-MISS no). No real infra or AI model output dependency is specified. Rejected Integration because behavior text is policy-level ranking logic, not cross-module state propagation.
behaviors.test_description: Feed the ranking policy function a candidate set with known browsed vs unbrowsed categories and assert top-N output includes at least one unbrowsed-category item.

behaviors.description: Cold-start users get popularity-based recommendations instead of empty results.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears when history lookup, fallback selection, and ranking path interact (MOCK-MISS yes). No real infra required; can be tested with local/in-process stores. No AI/ML nondeterminism required. Rejected Unit because behavior explicitly depends on fallback orchestration across modules.
behaviors.test_description: Use a user with no purchase/browsing records, execute recommendation service, and assert non-empty results are sourced/ranked by popularity signal.

behaviors.description: Click tracking stores product ID, list position, and timestamp for each clicked recommendation.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is between recommendation presentation context and tracking persistence schema (MOCK-MISS yes). Can be caught with in-process DB and controlled clock; no mandatory real OS/network/subprocess (REAL-INFRA no). Rejected System because behavior text is data handoff/persistence correctness, not external infra unpredictability.
behaviors.test_description: Simulate a click on a recommended item via tracking service, persist to local test store, and assert recorded row/event has exact product ID, rank position, and expected timestamp field.

behaviors.description: Recommendation generation stays under 200ms for 100,000 products.
behaviors.justification: This is a performance SLO dependent on real process/runtime characteristics (CPU, memory, runtime overhead), where mocks hide true latency (REAL-INFRA yes, MOCK-HIDE yes). Rejected Integration because in-process fakes or microbench stubs can miss real end-to-end timing bottlenecks.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real recommendation service process against a realistic 100k-product dataset and measure end-to-end request latency under controlled environment; assert p95 (or specified metric) is below 200ms.

behaviors.description: After a user purchases a recommended product, subsequent recommendations deprioritize that product.
behaviors.justification: Failure occurs across modules: purchase event/state update feeding the next recommendation ranking (MOCK-MISS yes). No required real network/OS behavior; local persistence and service wiring are sufficient. Rejected System because behavior text is state propagation and ranking update, not infra-specific behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Record a purchase for a previously recommended item, trigger next recommendation request for same user, and assert purchased item rank drops (or is excluded) relative to pre-purchase output.

behaviors.description: With unchanged user state, two recommendation requests return the same ranked list.
behaviors.justification: Determinism here is a pipeline property across filtering, ranking, and ordering stages (MOCK-MISS yes for module interaction), not just one function. No real infra dependency required, and no AI/ML nondeterminism is specified. Rejected Agentic because behavior text describes deterministic algorithmic recommendations, not real model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Execute recommendation service twice for the same user and identical snapshot state with no intervening events; assert full ranked list equality (IDs and order).
